title: Book Sport Transport
subtitle: Commercial-style iOS booking prototype
lead: A polished booking-style iOS prototype for sport transport services: catalogue browsing, customer details, pickup address, date/time selection, confirmation and map-style booking state.
card: A product-like transport booking flow with service cards, customer input, pickup details, time selection, confirmation and map-style booking status screens.
role: iOS Developer · UI Flow · Prototype Architecture
features: Transport/service catalogue with bike and scooter-style categories
Customer booking form with name, phone, pickup address and date/time slots
Booking confirmation state with selected transport and scheduled time
Map-style pickup/status screen for a customer-facing service experience
Client-pass-ready flow that can be extended with QR confirmation or digital access card
highlights: Structured a complete customer journey from discovery to booking confirmation
Designed production-style UI states around booking, customer details, route confirmation and client pass scenarios
Separated the experience into catalogue, booking and confirmation modules
Structured the flow the way a real product could use REST APIs, MapKit-based route context and QR-enabled confirmation
note: Source code is not public. The case study focuses on a commercial-style mobile flow centered on UIKit screens, customer journey, booking UX and production-style presentation. The product direction is well suited for REST APIs, Firebase-backed customer state, MapKit route handling and QR-based booking confirmation.

title: BC Game Space
subtitle: Gaming club booking and catalogue prototype
lead: A neon-style entertainment venue app prototype built around a game club experience: promo lobby, game space booking, console categories, curated game lists and tab-based navigation.
card: A commercial-style entertainment app with game-club booking, neon lobby screens, category filters, game lists and product-like navigation.
role: iOS Developer · UI Implementation · Product Demo
features: Branded loading and home/lobby screens with promotional cards
Game space booking screen with date/time-style booking context
Game catalogue grouped by categories such as arcade, pinball, manual games and consoles
Platform sections for Nintendo, PlayStation, Xbox, Retro and VR-style game booths
Tab navigation that simulates a full commercial mobile product
highlights: Built a polished entertainment app flow focused on visual identity and user journey
Designed content and navigation modules that can support remote catalogues, user sessions and live promotional content
Designed screens that can support booking, membership, QR pass, loyalty or player-profile modules
Kept the presentation strong while preserving a commercial product feel suitable for service-backed integrations
note: Source code is not public. This case study focuses on a commercial-style entertainment product with strong UI identity, content structure, booking-oriented flow and interaction states. In a production setup, the same direction can be supported by Firebase services, REST APIs, user profiles, loyalty flows and remote content delivery.

title: Habbitator
subtitle: Habit tracking app with Core Data and MVVM
lead: An iOS habit-building app where users choose three habits for the day, complete them, review calendar history and track progress/streaks.
card: A UIKit habit tracker with daily habit setup, progress screens, calendar history, local persistence and Clean Architecture + MVVM structure.
features: Create three daily habits and keep or replace them on a new day
Mark tasks as completed and track successful-day streaks
Review progress and completion history in a calendar
Switch between multiple visual themes
Store habits and daily completion records locally with Core Data
highlights: Split the app into App, Core, Domain, Data and Presentation layers
Controllers receive prepared screen data from ViewModels instead of reading Core Data directly
Repository protocol separates domain logic from persistence implementation
Demonstrates a stronger architecture than a purely screen-based demo
note: Public GitHub repository is available. The README describes Core Data storage, Clean Architecture + MVVM and UIKit implementation.

title: Forest Flicks
subtitle: Motion-controlled iOS archery game
lead: A landscape iOS archery game where the iPhone acts as a motion controller and a forward thrust fires an arrow at the target.
card: A landscape archery game with SpriteKit gameplay, arrow store, scoring multipliers, local progress and CoreMotion-based shooting gesture detection.
features: External-screen oriented gameplay concept with iPhone motion controls
CoreMotion shot gesture detection at high frequency
Store and arrow selection with persistent stock/balance
Target scoring zones with multipliers
Landscape game experience with custom visual assets
highlights: Implemented motion-based shooting using acceleration and rotation thresholds
Separated SpriteKit layout, gameplay, scoring and gesture detection responsibilities
Persisted progress through local storage while keeping the game lightweight
Designed a portfolio-friendly game concept that stands out from standard CRUD apps
note: Public GitHub repository is available. The README explains the CoreMotion shooting mechanics, scoring formula, local progress persistence and game module structure.

title: Follow Your Bet
subtitle: Personal sports analytics tracker
lead: A local-only personal tracking app for saving personal bet records, reviewing win/loss results, tracking profit/ROI and visualizing performance through dashboard screens.
card: A polished UIKit analytics app with onboarding, local records, dashboard cards, chart-based statistics, add-record flow and dark sports-style UI.
role: iOS Developer · Product UI · Local Analytics
features: Home dashboard with profit, ROI, recent records and donut chart
Add-record flow with validation, date picker and keyboard handling
Full list grouped by month/year and sorted by recency
Analytics screen with filters, category breakdown and top records
Fully local storage with UserDefaults and Codable
highlights: Structured UI, domain services and repositories with lightweight Clean Architecture
Built chart-based analytics screens using the Charts framework
Created a consistent dark visual identity and custom tab bar
Designed for personal tracking only — no bookmaker connection and no real-money transactions
note: Public GitHub repository is available. The README explicitly presents it as a personal tracking app and notes that it does not place bets, connect to bookmakers or process real-money transactions.

title: Daily List
subtitle: Checklist app with templates and themes
lead: A compact UIKit checklist app for creating personal lists, reusable templates, custom icons/colors, visual themes and archiving completed lists.
card: A UIKit checklist app with editable lists, templates, color/icon customization, themes, archive flow and UserDefaults + Codable persistence.
features: Create, edit and delete checklists
Add, edit, check and delete list items
Archive completed lists automatically
Create lists from built-in and user-saved templates
Customize list icons, accent colors and visual themes
highlights: Built table-driven editing and archive flows
Persisted lists, templates and theme state with UserDefaults + Codable
Created a lightweight design system for list colors and visual styles
Demonstrates clean UIKit fundamentals and local state handling
note: Public GitHub repository is available. The README positions it as a compact UIKit productivity app with table-driven editing, templates and theming.

title: Chickens & Beast
subtitle: Local multiplayer party-game prototype
lead: A local multiplayer party-game prototype where players receive hidden roles, move through daily events, discuss suspicious behavior, vote and try to survive while the Beast hunts the group.
card: A UIKit/XIB party game with hidden role assignment, player validation, event-driven turns, discussion timer, voting, eliminations and end states.
features: Local multiplayer flow for 5 to 9 players
Hidden role assignment with custom role cards
Player name entry and duplicate-name validation
Event-driven day progression, discussion and voting
Player elimination and win/lose end screens
highlights: Organized gameplay responsibility across main game, player selection, discussion and turn flow files
Modeled roles, events, durations and player state
Built a complete playable prototype with custom screens and assets
Good example of complex screen flow beyond standard list/forms
note: Public GitHub repository is available. The README documents the local multiplayer game loop, UIKit/XIB structure, role cards, events, timed discussion and voting flow.
